name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. The edge should come from deeper reset-and-reclaim participation rather than fragile one-bar continuation timing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if deeper reset entries are still too sparse, if reset zones mostly become full reversals, or if resumed continuation cannot overcome costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional swing pulls back deeply enough to reset positioning, then regains fast value and resumes in the original direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transitional markets often reject shallow continuation entries but still reward deeper resets that clear weak positioning before the next leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h offers cleaner support/reclaim structure than DOGE while remaining inside the allowed basket, making it better for separating reset depth from continuation confirmation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains fully rotational, deeper resets may bounce only to value and fail to continue.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade but runner performance is poor, keep the same entry logic and convert the back half into faster scaled exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reset depth, reclaim, and participation filter are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using a zone around EMA_55 instead of crash-only thresholds

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
